Relatedly, combinations of relatively obscure or seldomly paired genres (e.g., vaporwave polka , enka bluegrass or bluegrass dubstep) are more likely to result in chaos. Whether that’s a feature or a bug, we leave you to you to decide."

[2023-12-01 13:30:38] 317779427293396992: from the Terms of Service
> The Service is designed to process input data, information, and content that you provide in the form of code, video, images, information, data, text, software, music, sound and other audio, photographs, graphics, messages, and other materials and mediums (collectively referred to as the “Submissions”) and generate and return audio and visual output (“Output”, and together with the Submissions, “User Content”).

[2023-12-01 13:40:36] 317779427293396992: Here is what chatGPT said about it:
> The Suno Terms of Service distinguish between "Submissions" (the data and content you provide) and "Output" (the audio and visual output generated by Suno from your Submissions). [2023-12-01 13:40:39] 204320873719660544: > Service Description: The Service is designed to process input data, information, and content that you provide in the form of code, video, images, information, data, text, software, music, sound and other audio, photographs, graphics, messages, and other materials and mediums (collectively referred to as the “Submissions”) and generate and return **audio and visual output (“Output”**, and together with the Submissions, “User Content”).

with paid tier "Suno hereby assigns to you all its right, title and interest in and to Output"

Output is defined as audio and visual from Suno.
[2023-12-01 13:41:11] 317779427293396992: I think you are not getting my question. I'm asking if the Output is only the files or does it cover the musical composition as well?
[2023-12-01 13:42:33] 317779427293396992: ChatGPT again:
> If you wish to perform the music (i.e., the composition or arrangement created by Suno) live with a band, this might be seen as creating a new rendition or performance of that music. If the "Output" is interpreted to include the composition or arrangement itself, then as a free tier user, you would be allowed to perform it live, provided it's for non-commercial purposes and Suno is attributed. However, if the "Output" is strictly the specific audio/video files generated, then a live performance might be outside the scope of the license granted to free tier users.

[2023-12-01 16:47:33] 1002000139185045534: If you want your whole song to be sung by female singer only use [verse female vocals] and the throughout your song you will have same female singer
[2023-12-01 16:47:58] 204320873719660544: there are some gender/genre quirks using (adlibs) as call-and-response...

in funk it is usually a male singer (adlibs are female)
in jazz more often a female singer (adlibs are often male)

Broadway was usually female but it does duets really well.
[John]
[Mary]
[John and Mary]

[2023-12-01 22:22:29] 204320873719660544: tbh, your usage will change because you can spend the tokens when you need them to get completed songs...
(paid also gets commercial rights and you own the song)

Free is all the same features. but the wait for the reset means you probably don't try making longer songs because the moods not going to last over a couple days
(free is non-commercial+attribution)

so... it doesn't feel so much like the same thing, it goes from being a meme generator to being a song generator.... (the commercial rights feels that way to).

[2023-12-01 22:29:39] 204320873719660544: yes! you can Continue a chirp with another chirp. It listens to the end of the clip you choose, and generates another (up to) 60sec... (costs tokens)

you can change lyrics to start right where it cut off.
[2023-12-01 22:31:32] 204320873719660544: Do that a few times (Continue from a Continue, like a chain)

when you get to the end use Get Whole Song (free no tokens) to stitch all the clips together into 1 track

[2023-12-02 10:02:17] 614946962139250711: Any time you continue a song, it the music style should be a close match to whatever clip you are continuing from, even if you blank out all the text field prompts. (In fact it's quite difficult to drastically change the style, like convert a control song into EDM. Not impossible but you'll have to burn a lot of credits to get a lucky one to do it.)

[2023-12-02 11:24:16] 725830121751183493: [Instrumental Outro]

[2023-12-02 11:27:12] 204320873719660544: if you monetize your twitch, I would probably consider that commercial use...

with paid sub you own the songs and get commercial license. If you later un-subscribe or switch to free, the songs you made keep their commercial license forever...

Free subs can post on (free) social media with attribution to Suno (non-commercial license)

so my non-lawyer answer would be to subscribe for 1 month and do your show's songs, you own them forever and don't need to credit Suno...
before the end of month, drop to free until you need more.

[2023-12-02 23:52:43] 407677463548919828: The trick that worked best for me was style: `Christmas Carol, traditional, __preferred musical style__, vocal description`

So for my version of "We Wish You a Merry Christmas" I used `Christmas carol, traditional, bubblegum pop`

For my Jingle Bells I used `Christmas Carol, traditional, upbeat, tropical house, male vocalist`

You have to play with it, but it seems to mostly work well.

[2023-12-03 12:48:53] 380287720582283264: After sending your /chirp, the window show popup.

Type your genre in the top field.
Enter your lyrics in the second field.
If you don't have lyrics, type a subject in the bottom field, and it will generate lyrics for you.
If you put lyrics already, leave the bottom field empty.
If you put a subject in the bottom field, you can leave the middle field empty.

After all that, just click the blue "Submit" button

sometimes words that seem big ('death') don't do much, just shift to a minor key. 🤷‍♀️

[2023-12-03 20:50:04] 614946962139250711: In the current Discord version, the style is by default blank on a continue. In V2 I don't have a good sense of the best default, but I think it's probably similar, but also probably matters less. V2 continues are a lot more musically robust overall to *anything* in the style prompt compared to V1

[2023-12-04 14:19:31] 288134998941499393: Sumo.AI is struggling to generate my song. It keeps saying Credit refunded
[2023-12-04 14:24:17] 204320873719660544: listen carefully to the *end* of the clip you are continuing *from*...

The music may have 'ended' leaving a second or 2 of dead air. The continued clip hears the dead air, continues it for a few seconds, hears that nothing is playing and thinks it has finished.

It's a known issue and not always obvious from a casual listen. 

There's no workaround yet, but Suno is promising a feature to start a continue from a specific time (ie: start the Continue *before* the problem)...
[2023-12-04 14:28:39] 179015062122397696: > but Suno is promising a feature to start a continue from a specific time (ie: start the Continue before the problem)...

[2023-12-05 18:24:26] 204320873719660544: I listened to the start of some of my children's song tests... 

😅 That which has been heard cannot be un-heard... I didn't notice it before, lol

try
`children's sing-along but `*(your genre)*

that 'but' does some heavy lifting in Chirp:

`children's sing-along but funk`

[2023-12-06 03:54:38] 380287720582283264: Depends on how much you use the service.
Free users get 50 credits a day. (50*30= roughly 1500  credits per month)
The $8 plan gets you 2500 chirps per month, given all at once.
You don't have to wait day to day for more credits to reload.

It also gives you the rights to use your chirps commercially.
It gives you priority in the queue. (less wait time when generating.)
and it gives you access to purchase more credits. (which you can't do with the free plan)

[2023-12-06 07:22:35] 191050749256400896: Wholly AI generated works cannot be copyrighted.
As in, if you modify it, you can still copyright it.
Even in that court case, the composition of the images in the comic was copyrighted. But the images, which were stated to be entirely AI generated, were not.
[2023-12-06 07:22:44] 408658036694188032: I assume you mean that when you alter an ai generated piece of art you can copyright it
[2023-12-06 07:22:54] 404082634382114817: what do you think

[2023-12-06 07:24:03] 408658036694188032: And the grounds for the level of transformation of those works being enough to be considered altered is very shaky and needs more deliberation
[2023-12-06 07:24:38] 191050749256400896: The only ruling is on "wholly AI generated." It mentions nothing else.
[2023-12-06 07:24:41] 408658036694188032: Thats how public domain works though? If the original AI work is public domain, and edits of that work are derivative and can be copyrighted, then others can make derivative work of the original
[2023-12-06 07:27:33] 380287720582283264: Other people can make their own derivatives of the same original public domain work that you used, yes.
They can't make derivatives of your edited version.
[2023-12-06 07:27:34] 191050749256400896: Alright. So, if it were a work that was in the public domain, then yes. They could base it on the publicly available version. 
That doesn't mean that you can make reverse engineer whatever edits you assume someone did to an AI piece, and make it public domain.
[2023-12-06 07:28:25] 408658036694188032: It's also not necessarily true that just because it has not been ruled on, that derivative works can be copyrighted, since that is legal precedent that has not been yet covered
[2023-12-06 07:28:38] 408658036694188032: That would just be applying a fallacy of ignorance

With a bark so loud, and a wag so free,
Oh, what a sight to see!

**Chorus:**
Woof woof woof, under the moon so bright,
Sniff sniff sniff, in the cool of the night,
Wag wag wag, with the tail so light,
Jump jump jump, with all its might.

**Verse 2:**
Chasing its tail, round and round,
With a joyful leap and a playful bound,
In its happiness, we are found,
Oh, the joy that's all around!

**Chorus:**
Woof woof woof, under the moon so bright,
Sniff sniff sniff, in the cool of the night,
Wag wag wag, with the tail so light,
Jump jump jump, with all its might.

**Outro:**
Bark bark bark, as the day takes flight,
Bark bark bark, into the quiet night,
Bark bark bark, until the morning light,
Bark bark bark, oh what a delight!"
```

[2023-12-07 17:25:41] 549458893365968908: I use the Web UI not Discord. When you generate lyrics through the lyric generator. it has the chorus and verse in brackets. [Chorus]. However, you can go to chat gpt and tell it to put your formats in brackets. Verse, Chorus, Bridge etc. Then in the lyrics part i add things like [Harmony] [Harmonize] [Solo] etc at the top next to [Verse] etc. Then in style of music i put the main genre then other feels and sub genres. For example. Jazz, then I will put Dark jazz, Cabaret etc. or whatever other sub genre.
[2023-12-07 17:26:07] 407677463548919828: A better question might be "What is Motown Disco?"  You have a fixed idea in your head but I'm really not finding much about it.  I ended up resorting to asking GPT4 about it:

> Motown Disco isn't a widely recognized subgenre within disco or Motown, but the term might be used to describe music that combines elements of both Motown and disco. To explore the concept, let's break down the individual components.
> 
> Motown:
> Motown is a record label founded by Berry Gordy Jr. in Detroit, Michigan in 1959. It became the name of a genre that is characterized by its soulful vocals, catchy melodies, and distinctive rhythm section known as "The Funk Brothers." Motown had a huge influence on popular music, with artists like The Supremes, Marvin Gaye, and Stevie Wonder.
> 
> Disco:
> Disco is a dance music genre that emerged in the 1970s and is characterized by a steady four-on-the-floor beat, syncopated basslines, and string sections, alongside horns and electric rhythm guitars. This genre was primarily designed for dancing and gained mainstream popularity with hits from artists like Donna Summer and the Bee Gees.
[2023-12-07 17:26:14] 407677463548919828: > Combining Motown and Disco:
> In the late 1970s and early 1980s, as disco was on the rise, some Motown artists began incorporating the danceable beats and production style of disco into their music. This blending created a unique sound where the soulful and catchy hooks of Motown met the upbeat, dance-oriented beats of disco. The result was a body of music that could be referred to as "Motown Disco," characterized by:
> 
> Rhythm and blues roots with an emphasis on the pop structure and the soulful melodies typical of Motown records.
> The driving beat and up-tempo rhythms of disco, making the tracks suitable for discotheques and dance floors.
> The use of disco's orchestral strings and horns to add the lush, dramatic arrangements over the more traditional Motown sound.
> 
> An example of this blend can be found in songs like Diana Ross's "Love Hangover" or The Jacksons' "Blame It on the Boogie," which feature elements of both Motown and disco.
[2023-12-07 17:26:42] 407677463548919828: > As for how it differs from other types of disco, Motown Disco would hold onto the vocal stylings, songwriting, and melodic elements that were signature to the Motown label while adopting the rhythmic structure and instrumentation that is characteristic of disco music. Other types of disco might not emphasize the soulful vocals or be as focused on the strong storytelling found in traditional Motown music.

[2023-12-07 21:51:16] 549458893365968908: This is the prompt I use. Replace my intention with yours. You will notice my intention lol. 

Write the lyrics for a song about The dark mind of a Cabaret master. Be creative, use modern lyrical formats like Verse, Chorus, Bridge etc. Put the formats in brackets []. For example, [Chorus], then put any harmonies in (), for example Dreaming (Teaming). Make it all rhyme in a modern way. If you have any questions don't hesitate to ask.

[2023-12-07 22:39:05] 549458893365968908: What Style? How many or what is in []? [] really matters. Before you waste more credits. Know that what is in [] matters. I will sound like a record. But... [] really matters. Chorus and Verse are not the only things you can put in []. You can put [Bridge] [Hook] Solo] etc. And as i mentioned way above to someone else, do not i repeat, do not continue from a gen that has silence or close to silence at the end. Also take advantage of () Let's say you have a lyric let's say Born Free then you can put this in () (Now that I see) this will make the AI either harmoize that in () or just tell it to say it in spoken word, whisper etc.
[2023-12-07 22:39:22] 614946962139250711: You can try using putting tempo words (could just be adjectives, but also musical terms) in your starting style prompt and then making sure that style prompt is used in each future continue
[2023-12-07 22:40:11] 549458893365968908: You can add more than one style. Doesnt matter if the AI is trained on it. It will generate what is closest to what you input that it is trained on.
[2023-12-07 22:40:37] 614946962139250711: I also find using a repeating element at least every verse helps. A repeating callback, a line in every verse that is mostly the same, something that sort of keeps the music style consistent, bit less drifting.

[2023-12-08 07:38:25] 614946962139250711: Try simplifying the style prompt. Also may be good to use at least two versus of lyrics, though it should usually still work with one if you really want that. These are style prompts: (no commas. not sure about the commas in V2)
```
melodic electronic synthwave sultry female vocals
melodic synthwave sparse female 
melodic synthwave sultry female vocals
```
[2023-12-08 07:39:55] 238071088666509312: So the character "Asgore" from Undertale cannot be used in a prompt...The system sees "GORE" and is like...NOPE, CAN'T DO THAT!
[2023-12-08 07:39:56] 614946962139250711: In general if you have trouble getting Chirp to sing the lyrics, try describing a voice. "sultry female vocals" is what I used here. So any word that is often used to describe a voice in music can help.

[2023-12-10 04:13:25] 238071088666509312: The letter Ж (pronounced "zhe" in Russian) was not created by simply combining two Ks together. The letter Ж has its own distinct origin in the Cyrillic script, which is used for writing several languages, including Russian.

The Cyrillic script itself has evolved over time, and its early forms were influenced by the Greek alphabet. The letter Ж likely developed from the Greek letter Zeta (Ζ/ζ), but it has undergone changes in form and pronunciation to become a unique letter in Cyrillic.

While some letters in the Cyrillic script may have similarities to letters in the Latin alphabet or other writing systems, each letter has its own historical development and linguistic significance. The creation of the letter Ж involved a process of linguistic and script development rather than a simple combination of existing letters.

[2023-12-10 11:54:02] 641701655020044329: How do you guys prompt it better. I try to do music promp - Thin Lizzy "Boys are back in town" but do get different style
[2023-12-10 11:56:51] 204320873719660544: band names and musicians are mostly censored out for copyright reasons.

Try describing the genre and mood of the song (genre alone is a very broad description)
[2023-12-10 12:00:38] 641701655020044329: do you use , or ; which would be better for algo to recongse it ( ex. rock, uplifting) . Overall really do love it
[2023-12-10 12:13:34] 204320873719660544: use commas in the style. Items in a comma list are weighted to 'balance' – in theory a list of commas can be in any order and have equal influence 

(in actuality, some prompt-words are stronger than others and dominate the sound)

use square brackets as [prompts] in the lyrics,  chirp has been trained to see these as instructions.

use parentheses () for backing vocals and exclamations (ooh-yeah).

Other punctuation might work, and the llm will compensate for different punctuation if it understands the context, like I've pasted lyrics that use 
Verse: ... Chorus: ...
and it did he same it would if I'd used square brackets.

there are no special control tags or punctuation weights done by multiple brackets like {Bass:0.2} does nothing special, just confuses the bot or gets ignored.

(the random factor and lack of seed prevents hardcore testing with tiny changes in the prompt)

[2023-12-10 23:10:18] 979720704901677066: Not sure y'all are aware of how much the lyrics can affect the music style. I wrote a Christmas song and prompted for just "hiphop" for example. It had very little hiphop in it, which is usually an easy task for Suno.
[2023-12-10 23:17:07] 979720704901677066: Here is another example. Hiphop-inspired, maybe, but really not primarily would be considered hiphop. Because the lyrics are not hiphop it doesn't make hiphop.
[2023-12-10 23:32:17] 614946962139250711: You can stack up longer style descriptions and even just repeat words or phrases to make it stronger. Christmas lyrics *in particular* are insanely powerful though so to really make it distinct, generate your initial segment with pure hiphop throwaway lyrics and then continue it with your actual christmas lyrics. continues are sticker than style prompts, and you can use both at once in that second segment. Usually you don't have to do this but Christmas lyrics are a place you may
[2023-12-10 23:33:07] 1074658308499185684: #🐣┃chirp-beta-4
[2023-12-10 23:33:09] 614946962139250711: You can also hedge a bit. Make your firsr verse non-christmas, and the second verse your actual song, and edit in post

[2023-12-11 00:14:54] 614946962139250711: You can use [outro] and [fade out] both. Even if [fade out] *doesn't actually fade out* in my experience it still has an influence on that section of the song and tends to make it generate something more like the end of a song
[2023-12-11 00:15:45] 614946962139250711: The nuclear option for "the lyrics are too sticky on the music style" is to use your entire first segment with fake lyrics in the style you are aiming for and then just edit it in post.
[2023-12-11 00:17:02] 979720704901677066: I theorize (without knowing anything) that the AI can get confused when trying to fade out. [2023-12-11 00:18:22] 314598786448097280: Ah, thanks for the tip. I wish Intros were the same way. If you do two Outros in one clip, the first outro will simply extend the clip and the second one seems to end it, if luck is on your side. With Intros though, if I want an instrumental intro, I do:
[Intro}
(melodic instrumental, build)

Just doing instrumental intro just makes a long intro with no room for vocals inside of a minute. But I've had times where the build command is ignored. Most effective in parentheses it seems. Put it in brackets and it fails every time for me.

[2023-12-11 23:23:21] 814990167810113536: Yes, a lot. 
The lyrics can affect and influence determining things like Tempo, Flow, Energy, Mood, Voice etc.

Happy words tend to produce more happy songs, angry words more aggressive, lyrics formatted as a rap tend to be rapped, etc.
[2023-12-11 23:23:37] 814990167810113536: ^ just based on content of lyrics.

Also the amount of lyrics matters and will be important to get the right fit, making for best sounding song overall.

Too many lyrics = rushes
Too few lyrics = drags
Right amount = Hits!

++

Additionally, capitalization and punctuation will play into flow/mood/voice/energy.
[2023-12-11 23:30:35] 814990167810113536: Every character counts.
[2023-12-11 23:33:27] 407677463548919828: That and if your style predisopses it in that direction any audio descriptive words in the lyrics can push it towards that type of sound.
[2023-12-11 23:34:18] 407677463548919828: For instance I went for something in a slower sadder sound and directed for a lower pitch vocal, the fact my lyrics used 'whsipered' in the chorus conspired to creave a whispered vocal.
[2023-12-11 23:34:37] 814990167810113536: I can confirm this

[2023-12-12 15:02:15] 407677463548919828: A trick that I have had some success with is to have one of the GPTs break down the artist and a song I like into style and genres.  Then I'll try to reduce what it is giving me to a few words (because it is usually way verbose) and feed it back  to the GPT asking if this is correct or not.  Then I'll try to work on various aspects like vocal styles, instruments and playing styles, finally I'll try to see if I can find what parent styles their particular style may have dervived from.  Then I'll try to figure out a word salad that matches that and can fit in the character limits.

I'll try to either use my lyrics or a pastiche to see if I can describe the instrumentals as I go in the correct style and tweak it as I go.
[2023-12-12 15:03:29] 407677463548919828: A while back someone was asking about "Motown Disco" and I managed to get a description of "Melodic Disco" and "Soulful Vocal Disco" to work with from that.  Some of the people who were there have told me it's correct so I go with it when I make stuff in that style.
[2023-12-12 15:03:41] 407677463548919828: It's a bit of work but I think it can be done if you work at it.
[2023-12-12 15:03:51] 407677463548919828: The key is to experiment.

[2023-12-12 18:42:22] 407677463548919828: You should be able to see a menu next to the part you wish to continue. Then when you have completed the parts you need to make a complete song you would from that menu choose 'Get full song' on the part you wish to end with.

[2023-12-13 15:23:03] 204320873719660544: yeah... confusion with overlapping word meanings... 😅 

Suno's website calls individual 'chirps' a CLIP (we get 2 clips per generation)

then Suno calls the full continued sequence of 'chirps' a SONG (as in: Get Whole Song)

but in practice all the words are used here interchangeably....
[2023-12-13 15:27:42] 204320873719660544: I often do instrumentals that just continue without any waste chirps, so that's like 100%

with lyrics I always have some waste, and finding an ending is more waste... 

and not counting the waste that doesn't get continued at all...

[2023-12-13 15:54:01] 407677463548919828: As I understand it from personal use, each first Chirp is two options rendered at about eighty seconds long.  Then each continuation after that will be as much as sixty seconds or less depending on what you input and random chance.

So a first chirp plus one continuation would be about two minutes and twenty seconds.

Does that math make it easier for you ?

[2023-12-14 07:32:33] 238071088666509312: Can someone pin thie please?  I'm seeing WAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAY too much of this stuff.  

A common complaint about AI music generators: that they are making it too easy for people to create music, and that this is somehow ruining music.

There are a few things to consider here. First, it's important to remember that music has always been a collaborative art form. Musicians have always borrowed from and been inspired by each other. AI music generators are just another tool in that toolbox.

Second, AI music generators are not going to replace human musicians. They can't replicate the creativity and emotion that humans bring to music. What AI music generators can do is help people who are not musicians create music. This can be a positive thing, as it can allow more people to express themselves creatively.

Of course, there are also potential downsides to AI music generators. For example, they could lead to the homogenization of music, as everyone starts using the same AI tools to create the same kind of music. They could also lead to copyright infringement, as AI music generators can be used to create music that sounds like the work of other artists.

Overall, I think AI music generators are a positive development. They have the potential to make music more accessible and to help people who are not musicians create music. However, it's important to be aware of the potential downsides of this technology and to use it responsibly.

Here are some specific examples of how AI music generators are being used:

    Some musicians are using AI music generators to help them write songs. For example, the band Nine Inch Nails used an AI to help them write the song "Less Than Zero."
    Some music producers are using AI music generators to create backing tracks for their songs.
    Some people are using AI music generators to create their own personal soundtracks for their lives.

[2023-12-14 07:34:40] 1184076790235414540: I think it's positive too, but there are definitely some genres that are going to feel the effect of it more than others. [2023-12-14 19:15:22] 380287720582283264: Be as descriptive as you can be with with you want.
(i.e., don't just put "rock", or "rap")
Add in feelings that you want from the music..
[2023-12-14 19:15:48] 695968932359831562: awesome thank you (I don't have to put int: best quality, stuzdio quality etc ?)
[2023-12-14 19:16:13] 614946962139250711: The best advice I have is to not get obsessed with a specific song or style, stay as "wide" as possible in your Chirp exploration for a long time. It's real easy to burn a ton of credits getting tunnel visioned on one specific song or style and keep making tiny tweaks and pulling that lever over and over and over hoping to finally get it click. 

Don't just change a style word, change *all* the words, commas and no commas, musical terms and evocative adjectives. Don't just change some lyrics, change all the lyrics, all the meta tags, add or subtract a verse, try different punctuation, vary the line breaks. You'll learn a lot better and end up with a ton of clips you can build on for later continues.

[2023-12-14 22:38:22] 679888582168936461: For reference, I am more or less totally blind (no usable vision beyond lights and shadows). The current progression of AI has allowed for a vast array of services to be made available to us in 2023 alone. One of those is an app called Be My Eyes, wherein for a number of years its only real feature was to call a sighted volunteer that could assist you with various tasks (think reading cooking instructions on a frozen pizza or locating a delivery box on your front porch). In 2023, the app gained a new feature called Be My AI, which utilizes OpenAI's GPTVision model to describe photos in great detail and allows you to ask follow up questions. Cool, right? except... OpenAI's ethical bias dictates what is and isn't appropriate content to describe. Which means that the tool that's supposed to help us be more independent and gain a foot up in what is very clearly a predominantly sighted world can (and has) refused to work because a team of execs decided it was their place to dictate what we should and shouldn't have access to view.

[2023-12-15 07:16:16] 614946962139250711: For Bark tags like [laughs]
1. Some Bark voices may work perfectly with them, other not at all. Very voice dependent.
2. You may find some voices work in Chinese with the Chinese word for [laughs] (how it would be written on a Chinese TV subtitle, for example). Worth trying, it may work.
3. If you need to use a [tag] or set of [tags] in Bark for sure, often the best way is to generate random voices using the tags instead of using a prebuilt voice.
[2023-12-15 07:17:13] 614946962139250711: 4. If you just keep trying different voices, probably eventually you will find one that works well. For [laughs] it is not hard, that is a tag many voices do work well with.

[2023-12-15 09:46:50] 614946962139250711: One tool that is generally useful is to try repeating something to make it stronger. This doesn't work much better but *somewhat* better maybe: <https://app.suno.ai/song/4007b36c-0051-4963-958c-a0356f81e7da/>
```
[start]
...
[intro][intro][intro][intro]
[intro][intro][intro][intro]
...
```
Just a quick experiment
[2023-12-15 09:51:35] 614946962139250711: Actually you really have to use one line of real lyrics in front or it still gets skipped quite often.
```
[start]
[intro]
Maybe a line here?
... (...)
... (...)
... (...)
[intro][intro][intro][intro]
[intro][intro][intro][intro]
... (...)
... (...)
... (...)
... (...)
```

[2023-12-15 18:20:22] 549458893365968908: I formated this to work best with Suno. Hoever Chat GPT 3.5 ( the free chat gpt ) doesn't always get it right. Replace what I dsaid to generate with your subject matter.

Write the lyrics for a modern Christmas song about joy and giving, Be creative, be fun, use modern lyrical formats like Verse, Chorus, Bridge etc. Put the formats in brackets []. [2023-12-15 19:04:27] 549458893365968908: Use the prompt for chat gpt then copy and paste a verse, chours etc at a time. You will use less and less. [2023-12-15 21:07:46] 407677463548919828: There was a list somewhere that someone was working on but I don't know what came of it.

Basically when it comes to metatags you just have to try stuff and put it in brackets to see if it works.  If it works consistently (or consistently *enough*) then you  keep using it and it's a metatag.

Really even ones  that you're sure about can get unstuck at times.  More than once I've had it sing the words [electric guitar] even though other times it's happy to just call the instrument as asked for.
[2023-12-15 21:08:45] 282602192987357186: here's a post from user Sheep3D that gives an example of some meta tags that you can add. these are just examples, you can make your own or try new ones. there are likely many more that would work other than is listed here. I've only tried a few like [music] and [music outro] myself. https://discord.com/channels/1069381916492562582/1083129341870878780/1182093492026085406

[2023-12-16 12:18:14] 757310621577379931: The key differences between Ditto Music and RouteNote are in their services, pricing, and features:

**Ditto Music:**
- Offers extensive distribution deals and marketing tools for independent artists.
- Distributes music to over 150 stores and streaming services globally.
- Allows artists to control release dates, split royalties, and plan marketing strategies.
- Charges $19 per year for unlimited releases, with a 30-day free trial.


**RouteNote:**
- Provides free music distribution to major stores and streaming services.
- Assists in monetizing content on social media platforms.
- Offers music marketing tools through Push.fm.
- Provides detailed analytics and monthly payments via PayPal.
- Free users keep 85% of revenue, while premium users keep 100% for a small fee.

While both offer music distribution, Ditto Music includes more comprehensive promotion services and a flat-fee pricing model for unlimited releases. RouteNote offers free distribution services with a lower revenue share for free users and includes social media monetization tools.

[2023-12-16 15:07:31] 150492295962165248: **Title**: "Echoes of the Journey"

**Intro (Acoustic and Nostalgic)**
*(Soft acoustic guitar, echoing Johnny Cash's style)*
```
In the quiet of early dawn, under a vast open sky,
Dreams from nights long past whisper their secrets,
Roads stretch out, winding and endless,
Through fields where memories lie scattered like fallen leaves,
Here, every step is a silent story,
In the hush of morning, these tales begin to unfold.
```

**Verse 1 (Storytelling with Depth)**
*(Slightly faster tempo, introspective tone)*
```
On this path, footsteps echo beneath sun and moon,
Laughter and shadows mingle in the afternoon light,
Each stride reveals stories, old woven with new,
Through the tapestry of time, these narratives take flight,
Colors of life painted in every hue,
Under a sky ever-changing, from morning to night.
```

**Chorus (Uplifting and Catchy)**
*(Full band, combining Queen's grandeur with pop elements)*
```
Through storms and sunshine, this journey extends,
Dancing with time, where every moment blends,
Heartbeats form a melody, radiant and bright,
Under the cloak of night, our spirits take flight,
Our song weaves through the stars, a luminous thread,
In the symphony of the universe, endlessly spread.
```

**Verse 2 (Rap Element)**
*(Rhythmic rap, influenced by Eminem and Tupac)*
```
Miles unravel stories, voices from past and present,
Echoing through the corridors of time, insistent,
Scars and triumphs, worn with equal pride,
In this journey, our hearts refuse to hide,
Paths crossed, lessons learned, in each encounter a clue,
In every experience, our essence renews.
```

**Chorus**

**Bridge (Psychedelic Rock)**
*(Guitar solo, experimental sounds reminiscent of Pink Floyd)*
```
[Instrumental]
```

**Spoken Word Interlude (Theatrical)**
*(Reflective, akin to David Bowie's narrative style)*
```
Between the stars, silence speaks louder than words,
Here, we find our truth, in the unspoken heard,
Travelers not just in time, but in dreams unconfined,
Each verse a fragment of the soul, uniquely signed,
In this cosmic dance, our stories intertwine,
Revealing the essence of what it means to be alive.
```

**Verse 3 (Melodic and Reflective)**
*(Melodic style, inspired by Fleetwood Mac)*
```
Horizon whispers my name, in a golden embrace,
Life's constant change, a dance of grace,
Mirrored in the sky, dreams take flight,
Soaring on wings of dawn, into the night,
Every end is a beginning, in time's endless chase,
In the heart of the journey, we find our place.
```

**Chorus**
*(Key change for a climactic effect)*

**Outro (Classic Rock Fading Out)**
*(Electric guitar blended with modern production elements, fading gradually)*
```
To roads untraveled, our hearts call,
In the echoes of our journey, we find our all,
Stories spun under the setting sun, ever so bright,
In these echoes, we find our light,
Onward we roam, in the heart's quest,
In the echoes of the journey, we find our rest.
```

[2023-12-17 05:00:05] 407677463548919828: Quoting myself from another time this happened:

> Try depending on where you are in your lyrics (if you have a lot more to go) just continuing and running the same lyrics you have.  This can sometimes make it pick up where it left off.  A large percentage of the time it will start over in the middle of the song.  This is just the easiest thing to try to see if it works.
> 
> Next try removing everything already sung but the last good line, then try continuing.  This sometimes works.  It's how it always seemed to work in v1 Chirp for me.
> 
> After this try creating a line break (empty space) and continuing from the line that was interrupted.  This has a good chance of working.
> 
> Then starting with the line that was broken.  It sometimes will "remember" where it was and pick up from there.
> 
> Finally when all else fails, batten down the hatches, remove everything up to the word that was interrupted and hope for the best and throw the dice.  You will likely encounter some weirdness and you will need to keep trying.  But if everything else has failed it's this or...
> 
> ...or you go back up a chain in the song and try again giving up on the good part.  It hurts, I know, I know.  But sometimes it's the only way forward.
> 
> ...As you can probably tell this is a bit of a pain point for me and I've done these often...
[2023-12-17 05:00:25] 407677463548919828: It's a bit of a process but it *can* be saved.
[2023-12-17 05:01:28] 1184076790235414540: good to know! trial and error and error 😉

[2023-12-17 11:10:34] 1185894976647864362: Título: "No Bar da Solidão"

Verso 1:
No balcão do bar, eu me perco na bebida,
A saudade entranhada, a ferida que ainda lateja.
O copo na mão, o álcool a me envolver,
Em cada gole, o amor que se foi parece esquecer.

Refrão:
Vou me embriagar, afogar na sofrência,
Na triste melodia, a lembrança da sua ausência.
Entre um gole e outro, a dor a se perder,
No bar da solidão, tento esquecer você.

Verso 2:
O uísque desce amargo, igual ao gosto da saudade,
A música sertaneja, a trilha da minha ansiedade.
Os olhos marejados, as lágrimas que rolam,
No bar da desilusão, as lembranças me devoram.

Refrão:
Vou me embriagar, afogar na sofrência,
Na triste melodia, a lembrança da sua ausência.
Entre um gole e outro, a dor a se perder,
No bar da solidão, tento esquecer você.

Ponte:
No canto do bar, um cantor solitário,
A guitarra chora, traduzindo meu calvário.
O copo se esvazia, a noite fica mais fria,
No bar da saudade, a sofrência é minha guia.

Verso 3:
A cada acorde, sua imagem ressurge,
O cheiro do álcool, as lembranças me insurgem.
Os risos que se perderam, no silêncio a ecoar,
No bar da solidão, a sofrência a me abraçar.

Refrão:
Vou me embriagar, afogar na sofrência,
Na triste melodia, a lembrança da sua ausência.
Entre um gole e outro, a dor a se perder,
No bar da solidão, tento esquecer você.

Final:
O barman me olha, conhece minha história,
O copo vazio, reflete a minha memória.
No bar da saudade, sigo nessa dança sem fim,
Na sofrência que embala, o lamento do meu querer por mim.

.
.
if so, that could potentially be one part of the cause of the "robotic"-ness of the voice.
it's like you didn't give it any instruction and so over time it will "fall off" and revert to... robotic?

where you have just "2000's alternative" for your example, might not be enough for it to get Un-Robotic.

[2023-12-19 06:59:52] 824959191101472800: That is, is it like the option to continue for this clip but making a new one?
[2023-12-19 07:01:00] 824959191101472800: It's what gives you the option to choose a song, and not all of them are there.
[2023-12-19 07:01:06] 1184076790235414540: it would make a new one if you haven't selected a clip to continue from.
[2023-12-19 07:01:36] 1184076790235414540: basically, all that remix is doing is copying over the text you put in and nothing else, whereas continue from clip uses the clip you choose and works from there
[2023-12-19 07:02:07] 1184076790235414540: so like if you have a prompt for instrumental stuff that gives good results sometimes, you might want to go back and get that prompt to do some more outputs, so 'remix' makes that a little easier than copying it manually

[2023-12-19 07:06:27] 614946962139250711: Remix = Reprompt.
[2023-12-19 07:07:08] 824959191101472800: Respect style, rhythm, voice, etc?
[2023-12-19 07:07:46] 614946962139250711: No. Remix = "Reuse the text prompts. Just the text prompts."
[2023-12-19 07:08:31] 614946962139250711: I originally suggested renaming it to "Reuse this text prompt" but it go renamed back to remix in latest update, lol. As the person answering questions I always prefer the most extra super clear names lol
[2023-12-19 07:09:42] 614946962139250711: I think "Reprompt" is a good compromise though. "Remix" is just particularly misleading because of the specific meaning in a music context.

[2023-12-20 01:46:25] 407677463548919828: There is also a possibility that it could be triggered for other reasons than censorship.

And OpenAI can tilt at the silliest things.  I had it reject a song I was working on because the lyrics had the singer say someone was "so stupid, so so stupid" and refuse to go any further.  By the time it unlocked itself the inspiration had left.

[2023-12-20 03:42:14] 204320873719660544: No, idk how the copyright law works in your country, but in the US (where Suno is) the music does not get a copyright because it was not human authored. 

If you use your own lyrics, your words get copyright (that's all).

with a *paid* subscription you get a commercial license and 'all rights' to use the song (live perform, make derivatives, sell). The license is important for music publishing and distribution, not the copyright. Copyright is who wrote the song, License is who owns the song. Suno transfers the rights to the song through the license.

(free subscription, Suno keeps the rights and they own the song)

[2023-12-20 06:16:40] 614946962139250711: ### intro prompts that at least occasionally worked for me:
```
[intro]
[instrumental]

[intro]
[instrumental interlude]

[intro]
[instrumental]

[intro]
[instrumental hook]

[intro]
[interlude]

[build]
[melodic instrumental]

[melodic instrumental]

[intro] [intro] [intro]

[intro][intro][intro]

[intro] [intro] [intro]
[intro] [intro] [intro]

[intro] [intro] [intro]
[intro] [intro] [intro]
[intro] [intro] [intro]
[intro] [intro] [intro]

```
**If Chirp keeps skipping over anyway, try adding a bit of sung lyrics in front like this:**
```
(ooh-yeah)
[intro]
[instrumental]
```

1. Click Generate (10 credits)
2. Two clips are made.
3. If one clip happens to be a refund, you spend 5 credits.

But this is not typica.

[2023-12-20 19:30:24] 272500834175025153: Quick question what do you use for De-clipping? Or to repair the sound from being muffle sometimes @Marlowne
[2023-12-20 19:30:32] 549458893365968908: For example... Just replace what I told it to do with what you want.

Write the lyrics for a progressive rock song about There's Always Hope, this is the title track of an album so make it epic, Be creative, be fun, use modern lyrical formats like Verse, Chorus, Bridge etc. Put the formats in brackets []. For example, [Chorus], then put any harmonies in (), for example Dreaming (Teaming). Make it all rhyme in a modern way. If you have any questions don't hesitate to ask.

[2023-12-20 21:40:50] 204320873719660544: so there's a pretty common issue where chirp 'ends' the song abruptly but leaves a second of dead air, and when we try to continue the next clip continues dead air, then thinks it's finished and ends the clip... that's why the refund is automatic if the clip is just a few seconds.

It's hard to be sure (?) but I think this clip has a tiny amount of dead air at the end (it was cut short, clips should be longer) and that may be what's killing the continue... 😞 

There is a feature coming pretty soon (a month?) which should allow us to Continue from a specific time... idk if this clip can be revived once that feature is live... I haven't seen it yet.

It is a great sound... I'm sorry I don't have better suggestions....

(My preventative trick is to add something extra to the end of my lyrics, like
[Bass Drop]
or
[Break]
)

[2023-12-21 09:51:19] 686716515856154624: @keenan Hi! The new website is great! I just checked the TOS there, and there's a contradiction I wanted to point (quite a common one, MidJourney made the same originally):

Free users are supposed to not use their songs for commercial use. But Non Users are not bound by the TOS, and they can!

Why? Machine generated songs are public domain, just as machine generated images and text have been repeatedly found to be public domain by default (no human authors. In custom mode,  lyrics **could** be protected as the user's own artwork, so users could own copyright on lyrics. But let focus on non-custom mode for now)

Each time a (non-custom) song  is shared publicly, for example on Instagram or TikTok, Suno **non-users** can legally use them for commercial usage (as they are public domain). But the original user can't!

So there’s an asymmetrical burden on respectful free-tiers users, tied by the TOS (but TOS can be illegal of course, especially in the EU) and no burden at all on non-users!

What do you think? I of course understand the need to incentive paid-tiers, but gen AI creates a new type of content that is by default free of copyright and public domain, and it creates these new interesting weird legal cases.

[2023-12-21 14:16:30] 1134508628959838249: “The AI ​​wrote us a song about the end of the war in Gaza Copilot, Microsoft's chatbot, allows users to create songs using a text command - a new capability based on Suno's technology. We were favorably impressed by the musical skills of the new instrument, but the line "A new light shines beyond the bicycle" shows that more work needs to be done on its abilities in Hebrew”

Microsoft's chatbot, Copilot (formerly Bing Chat), now allows users to create songs using artificial intelligence. The new ability is based on a plug-in from Suno, an AI tool for creating music. "Through this partnership, people will have the ability, even without any musical background, to create fun, smart and personalized songs using a prompt (textual command - YM)," says Microsoft.

To create new music yourself, you must connect to Copilot at this address using the Edge browser (the ability is currently not available in the Chrome browser), activate the Suno plugin in the Plugins menu and then click on the Make music with Suno button. Now you can ask it to create a song for you using a text command. A specific theme, style or language can be requested. The result can be shared with friends on social networks.

We tried the plugin ourselves and were pleasantly surprised by its level: the music is coherent and has a distinct style, and the vocals don't sound like gibberish unlike previous models we've heard. Another surprise awaited us when we asked Copilot to create a song in Hebrew for us about the end of the war in Gaza: the chatbot answered the request and created a song for us in an oriental style, including the frills. The only problem was the strange text that included lines like "Silhouette bombs began to weave completely", "A new light shines beyond the bicycle" and "We have hope that after this failure Israel will return to peace" (listen to the full song in the player above).

https://www.ynet.co.il/digital/technews/article/s15ots11wt
[2023-12-21 14:17:07] 1134508628959838249: I am really happy that suno will come to everyone with Microsoft. 

Suno really deserves it!
[2023-12-21 14:18:26] 1134508628959838249: Continue article:

It is important to note that the model does not produce full songs, but segments up to a minute long. Microsoft also limits the number of songs you can create each day. After two songs, Copilot politely asked us to try again tomorrow.

After artificial intelligence has taught us to question every image or text we see on the web, there is no doubt that its next front - or at least one of them - is the music arena. Microsoft and Suno are certainly not the first to work in this field: last month, Google unveiled Dream Track, a new feature of YouTube shorts, which allows users to write a short description of a song and receive a 30-second piece of music performed by well-known singers who have authorized Google to reproduce their voices. Meta and OpenAI have also presented models for creating music using AI in recent years, but they have not yet released them for use. It is likely that in the coming months we will see more and more such models reaching the hands - and ears - of the users

free subscription gets an in-house (personal, non-commercial) license. Ok to post on free social media, but you must give Suno credit (and Suno owns the song)

[2023-12-21 14:34:15] 154320513626275840: is it not possible to delete songs from the create-list on the website? 🤔
[2023-12-21 14:35:05] 204320873719660544: hello, (sorry for my English)

you do not need to delete an old song to create a new song, but free account have a limited number per day (50 credits = 5 chirps)
Credits refresh each day, but do not roll-over.

Possibly you mean the continue function is only continuing from the same clip? there is a [CLEAR] button to remove that clip and create a fresh song (not continue)
[2023-12-21 14:36:48] 204320873719660544: the down-thumb hides clips from the Create pages... but they still show up in your Library.

(no one else can see them if you don't share the link)

I am not sure if a full delete is coming as a future web UI feature...

[2023-12-21 17:23:39] 488071734893871114: I may have missed the answer to this question somewhere, but are there specific keywords or commands you can include in your lyrics to indicate things like a fade to end, or a bridge?

[Bridge]

[2023-12-21 18:20:52] 313741476951752705: I managed to do it by using the "..." > "Continue from this clip" in the next iteration you will be able to make the full song by pressing "..." > "get whole song"

[2023-12-21 18:41:35] 204320873719660544: As @julienreszka said, it is same menu... under [...]
the options come and go depending on the clip that is *currently* selected...

so, clip 1 choose Continue...
(costs tokens)
pick the result and again on Clip 2 click the [...] and pick Continue... etc

or now there is a new option to Get Whole Song
(free, no tokens)
this will stitch all the clips together into 1 seamless song.

[Intro]
Line one
Line two
Line Three
Line four

Same with Chorus, verse etc.

[2023-12-21 19:24:16] 549458893365968908: Your not limited to Verse and Chorus. Use Bing Chat to generate a full structured written song. Use your own lyrics and tell Bing Chat to format it inthe way you want. Here is an example.

Write the lyrics for a Southern Rock, American Folk, Rockabilly, song about how peace in the world in not only possible but vital to the survival of Humanity, Be creative, be fun, use modern lyrical formats like Verse, Chorus, Bridge etc. Put the formats in brackets []. For example, [Chorus], then put any harmonies in (), for example Dreaming (Teaming). Make it all rhyme in a modern way. Also make sure it is unique in every way. If you have any questions don't hesitate to ask.

[2023-12-22 09:29:58] 702186550452027473: is there any way to make the songs longer than 1:20?
[2023-12-22 09:36:44] 204320873719660544: click the [...] button, and choose Continue From This Clip to (add up) to 1min more
(costs tokens)

Edit your lyrics to pick up where they left off.

you will use the [...] button again and Continue from Clip 2, to make Clip 3... and so on, continuing one clip after another.

At the end of the song, on the last clip use the [...] button and pick Get Whole Song to stitch all the clips together
(free, no tokens)

 extremely realistic cartoon universe,{insert your own scene here on what you want to happen in image},,,animation,,perfect face,perfect hands,fantasy picture,Fantastic,'high detailed snapshot, photo realistic, vivid, epic detail,vivid,detailed body,video game,,unreal engine,,

or this
heavy metal
surreal, fantastical realm. The imagery is characterized by vibrant, mythical landscapes, exaggerated and fantastical creatures, and a distinct aesthetic reminiscent of classic heavy metal album covers. [2023-12-22 17:15:40] 718127611016118272: Does anyone know how to create a song that is 3 minutes long? It seems that suno.ai only allows me to generate songs up to 1:30 in length.
[2023-12-22 17:15:47] 469164793660702730: It’s the command
[2023-12-22 17:16:53] 469164793660702730: Click the side tab then continue  with more lyrics, i do 4-6 sentences each generation plus the chorus/ending/or intro
[2023-12-22 17:19:06] 469164793660702730: For instance in the first section I want to generate I would write


[intro]
Yeah you know Mary, she had that little lamb
But let me tell you about Sam, Sam I am 


[verse]
No I don’t like green eggs and ham
I like spam and my name is Sam
If you give me your meaty
I won’t be needy
Baby I’m just greedy
I’ll let you please me
Like you know you can
[2023-12-22 17:19:42] 718127611016118272: found it! thank you guys.
[2023-12-22 17:20:07] 204320873719660544: Edit your lyrics to pick up where they left off.

you will use the [...] button again and Continue from Clip 2, to make Clip 3... and so on, continuing one clip after another.
Each continue costs tokens)

At the end of the song, on the last clip use the [...] button and pick Get Whole Song to stitch all the clips together
(free, no tokens)

[2023-12-23 02:13:21] 116357530111836160: You can make a longer song by making the first section and then choosing to Continue From This Clip.  When you are done making all the sections that way, Get Whole Song.  Both of those options should be available by clicking the three dots on the right side, on the website. [2023-12-23 02:20:30] 380287720582283264: The "10 running jobs at once" part of a subscription means you can have ten generations going at once, without waiting for them to completely generate.
Each job will have two songs.
You don't ten versions of a song from a single prompt.

Also, subscribing doesn't give you the ability to create longer songs.
You still have the same character limit as everyone else, and you still need to  [continue] your clips to make them longer.

But it's not a tool for "higher quality" in a general sense. In fact I feel like I'm typically trading away quality to get to the weirder parts of model, or at *risking* quality when I prompt like that.
[2023-12-23 09:05:27] 614946962139250711: I do think it's a good tool to keep in your belt. Even as simple as, "I'm not getting what I want. What if I just toss a totally random weird word in the style field and roll the dice. At least it might be different..."

[2023-12-23 09:26:18] 614946962139250711: i think (read: my non expert guess) entropy is more relevant for under the hood stuff we can't access, the tokens/prompts of the raw music generation. a regular text llm inputs text and generates additional text, token by token. an audio llm input text but you can't decode it's output back into text. it's operating and generating tokens that represent sounds, musical concepts, voices, *and* some sense of word meanings, a lot more things. (we don't know how chirp works but in bark this would be for example semantic tokens verses the text input) you could try and listen to the words it outputs and write them back down as text, but that's a lot more indirect. (it is however a great way to *write* chirp lyrics( https://discord.com/channels/1069381916492562582/1083129341870878780/1165328394876092488)
[2023-12-23 09:26:50] 614946962139250711: however you really do *have* to get extremely weird with text prompts to even get some whole categories of chirp, 100%. entropy is critical in this sense. actually please @ me any strange prompts you discover that do something interesting, can never get enough. but at least for me I often struggle with music or audio quality as I crank it way way up to find these places.

[2023-12-23 13:07:32] 914918494305407017: hello, I'm making songs, I'm making songs piece by piece and mixing them, but I can't adjust the beginning of the track and the end of the track. how can I do this?
[2023-12-23 13:14:20] 204320873719660544: you can maybe prompt and ending with 
[Fade and end]
or 
[Outro] and you do the actual fade in a DAW

Intro is hard because there is no way to 'in-paint' music that leads into your main section...
I suggest maybe creat a clip that uses 
[percussion drop]
or some other form of musical break, that would then build up, that you could use as an intro...
There is probably a music term for when the music drops to silence and builds from nothing, but I haven't found it yet

[2023-12-23 14:41:59] 335068690607243264: hi can someone please tell me what are the benefits of subscribing to this bot, i dont mean to sound mean if i do i just want to know what the difference is from being a subscriber and not
[2023-12-23 14:48:52] 204320873719660544: quality and features are the same (this might change as new features are added, but free has always been the full-quality)

Free has 5 chirps/day, suitable for memes and (non-commercial) social media (with attribution to Suno)

Paid get their monthly chirps all at once, so it's better for full-length songs. You also get commercial license and all rights to the song (spotify, apple music, bandcamp, etc)

[2023-12-23 17:07:44] 303153893448351746: When you continue from a clip, does the part 2 not include the first part? Do I have to combine both files together myself?
[2023-12-23 17:10:49] 204320873719660544: on clip 2 hit the [...] and click Continue From... again
so clip 3 is continued from clip 2
... keep chaining them one after the other, until you find the ending you like.
on that last clip, use the [...] and pick Get Whole Song.

it costs tokens to add more clips, but getting the whole song is free, and you can do it on all the endings you like, or at any point just to hear your progress.
[2023-12-23 17:11:15] 204320873719660544: edit your lyrics on each clip...
[2023-12-23 17:11:28] 204320873719660544: to pick up where the last one left off.

[2023-12-23 17:12:35] 614946962139250711: In case this didn't work, you can run this code in browser console before clicking "Sign Up" and it should make the entire sign up popup menu readable to the screen reader:
```
document.querySelector('button').addEventListener('click', () => {
    const checkAndRemoveAttribute = setInterval(() => {
        const element = document.querySelector('.cl-internal-1i1clgr');
        if (element) {
            element.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
            clearInterval(checkAndRemoveAttribute);
        }
    }, 100); 
});
```

[2023-12-24 00:45:20] 116357530111836160: One thing to consider is that different countries have very different ways of dealing with copyright. [2023-12-24 14:31:59] 614946962139250711: I think you can do it now but it requires too many rerolls to be practical unless you have infinite money. You'll want each song to be at least two clips away from the core segment I think, or else each song will be too similar. But you'll have to a lucky style drift where the song melody and maybe instruments change but the *voice * does not change. That is possible but not common at all. You'll also run into quality issues since the songs start farther up the tree with higher risks of audio problems. Another problem you could probably avoid with infinite money and time but may not be practical.

[2023-12-24 17:05:18] 204320873719660544: as long as you had a paid subscription at the time the clip was made, you can give it to someone else, edit it, make derivatives... you own the song. 

A lot of those content/copyright strikes are automated... the big labels protect their interest (they have to).

But there is no 'library' of Suno/AI-generated music... an automated content search wouldn't work because these songs are not in any database.
[2023-12-24 17:07:26] 204320873719660544: Music is about the license.

[2023-12-24 17:07:49] 204320873719660544: So all the legal stuff is based on licensing

[2023-12-24 18:27:06] 501404953865093150: on suno.ai, how to make song longer than 1:20?
[2023-12-24 18:31:09] 204320873719660544: use the [...] button, pick Continue From This Clip
it will open the Custom editor where you can change the lyrics for Clip 2

pick the better for Clip 2, use [...] again and Continue From Clip 2 to make clip 3
...and continue from clip 3 to make clip 4...
(each costs tokens)

on the clip that is the 'ending' use [...] and pick Get Whole Song
it will stitch all the clips together into 1 song
(free, no tokens)
[2023-12-24 18:42:15] 97754543919489024: or, with your generated clip you can click the 1 or 2 emoji to continue the 1st or 2nd option, and add more lyrics or w/e you want, then when youre content you can use the heart emoji to stitch them all together into a single clip
[2023-12-24 18:51:51] 354474826192388127: this isn't possible on the suno webapp right?

This error means your text request was blocked. Credits are refunded for rejected prompts. 

- Your **Lyrics** are copyrighted
- Your **Style of Music or Song Description** uses a real Band or Artist
- Your **Lyrics** contain Naughty Words or are Overly Explicit in general
- You’re actually O**ut of Credits** but the web page is out of sync. (Refresh the page.)
- In **Simple Mode** or the “**Make Random Lyrics”** button, the Lyrics AI can’t understand how to turn your request into a song.
- **The Moderation System Is Down -** Check in Discord if everyone else has the same problem.
- A **Moderation Mistake**. It happens. Sometimes a small tweak resolve the issue.

If you think it's a Moderation Mistake, you can submit your prompt to this unofficial [Google Form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1I6B1GyBSMV3apIaxJMvgZ19nHwFUzUFieCmOoL2s274/) to help improve the moderation system. (This isn't an official Suno report, just a form setup by Discord mods.)

[2023-12-25 14:29:31] 204320873719660544: the problem is the clip you are continuing *from*... the music has ended before the audio, and there's a half-second of dead air at the end.

The next clip continues with some dead air, after a few seconds hears the clip is silent and ends the audio.
It's a known issue, that's why your clips are refunded.

the only fix (for now) is to go BACK to an EARLIER clip, and regen from there. 

(A coming feature should allow us to continue from *time*, so we can continue a clip from before a problem spot in the audio... no eta on that feature going live)

[2023-12-25 16:14:25] 204320873719660544: It's a generic error message. afaict GPT doesn't send back any details why the prompt didn't go through, so the error message is intended to 'cover the most likely' reasons...
(it is very misleading, if it's a weird glitch like you are getting on an obviously innocent prompt)

my workaround is to add blank space(s) to the front (and end) of the text prompt... for some reason this changes the token values on GPT, but doesn't change the token values on Chirp (it will sing the same)...

Suno devs know this is an issue, they are trying to solve it...

[2023-12-25 18:28:06] 1101655905411530843: I made it with chat GPT
[2023-12-25 18:31:24] 204320873719660544: on the first clip click the [...] button and choose Continue From This Clip
(costs credits)

on the second clip, click [...] again and Continue From... again, to make clip 3
and on clip 3 to make clip 4... etc

on the last clip, click [...] and choose Get Whole Song to stitch all the clips into 1 seamless song.
(free, no credits)

The continued clip can by 1:00 at most.

[2023-12-26 17:01:15] 1057626425584267294: Well as I think about prompts in a particular way, I thought I'd write some different ones down and get your feedback. I've tried to place them into the only two places they can go; Lyrics and Style. 

I haven't tried them yet but I will. 

🔍 Question: Can I leave Lyrics Box empty and get an instrumental piece?

🔍 Question: Is there a limit to the number of words/characters that you can use in both Lyrics and Style?

Genre-Based Prompt: "Create a lively pop song about celebrating good times with friends."
        Lyrics: celebrating good times with friends.
        Style: Create a lively pop song
        
        Mood-Focused Prompt: "Generate a calming acoustic melody suitable for a peaceful evening."
        Lyrics: ?
        Style: Generate a calming acoustic melody suitable for a peaceful evening
        
        Thematic Content Prompt: "Compose a rock anthem about overcoming challenges and achieving dreams."
        Lyrics: overcoming challenges and achieving dreams
        Style: Compose a rock anthem 
        
        Instrumental Style Prompt: "Produce an electronic dance track with heavy bass and synthesizers."
        lyrics: ?
        Style: Produce an electronic dance track with heavy bass and synthesizers.
        
        Seasonal Prompt: "Write a cheerful summer song with upbeat rhythms and joyful lyrics."
        lyrics: cheerful summer song 
        Style: upbeat rhythms
        
        Nostalgic Prompt: "Create a 1980s-style ballad about reminiscing old memories."
        Lyrics:  reminiscing old memories.
        Style: Create a 1980s-style ballad
        
        Experimental Prompt: "Generate an avant-garde piece blending jazz elements with modern beats."
        Lyrics?
        Style: Generate an avant-garde piece blending jazz elements with modern beats

[2023-12-26 18:00:22] 1033422088461090827: https://youtu.be/Ma4C9wTppaU?si=GJR7HZWGKsm0OBvG
[2023-12-26 18:02:51] 203234235522351105: in style, add "genre, female/male vocals".. it's not that adding "CHORUS:" to the lyrics will make it the same. What doing that does is separates the parts for parsing.. as in, it'll make the timing better or add a solo if the prior lyrics didnt makeup good timing for the next BAR to begin - sometimes if I don't seperate the parts it will SPEED THRU he lyics trying to squish them all into 4 bars when they should be two parts , for example

[2023-12-26 18:16:48] 1075794537731076177: What causes that, from a technical perspective?  Just curious…
[2023-12-26 18:22:23] 711291862777856120: DEAR GOD ITS COOKING
[2023-12-26 18:26:26] 204320873719660544: i think it's the same idea as roleplay or extended chat with a chatbot... the bot can remember a certain amount of history, but starts forgetting older parts of the conversation as it goes on, and that makes the bot/character 'drift' – same with Chirp, it needs to sing the chorus every so often to remember it... and after a long instrumental you won't get the same voice as before...

(and sometime Chirp will re-sing a whole section un-prompted)

Dev's have said there is actually more memory on v2 than is currently being used, so they have ideas how to make it remember the beginning of the track (that is usually the best part) so the later continuations can sound more like the beginning.... (how this will work as a ui, I'm not sure has been worked out yet...)

[2023-12-27 14:11:45] 1057626425584267294: Has anyone tried using 'Continue from this Clip' as 'Edit this Clip'? 

I mean that rather than just extending the song, write 8 lines of whatever, and then see what you can do with them, such as change voice, add or change lyrics, modify using square brackets, etc.

I'm trying to modify a track one step at a time, as you would if you were sitting there composing a song without Suno and not have it create an entirely new composition each time.

Hope that makes sense!

(free, no tokens)
Chirp will stitch all the clips together into 1 seamless song.

I've made Custom GPT to make songs: "Suno Song Composer by MichalAI", requires OpenAI ChatGPT Plus. Someone would like to try beta?
Main features:
- Rewrite existing songs -> Makes another song containing same word count, rythm, syllabs, accents, template (verse-bridge-chorus-instrumental)
- Song Style -> Describes style by existing artist name or his specific songs
- Continue -> Paste current song lyrics and creates next parts up to end, to make it faster
- If you want, it adds params and enhanced meta tags
- And of course makes full songs
Looks nice, it's really useful for me.
Devs: Awesome job. I'm totailly addicted to your awesome idea!

[2023-12-28 11:38:18] 1057626425584267294: Suno AI uses a combination of NLP models for lyric generation and machine learning models for music composition. These models work in tandem to create songs that are not just technically sound, but also creatively compelling. The technology represents a fascinating blend of AI's analytical capabilities and artistic expression. 

The above is a summary:
[2023-12-28 11:39:10] 1057626425584267294: Below I've tried to add a bit more detail. Hope this helps. 

Interactivity of Models: In a system like Suno, the models used for lyric generation and music composition are usually designed to interact with each other. This means that the output of one model can influence the output of another. For instance, the mood or theme of the lyrics generated by the NLP (Natural Language Processing) model can guide the style or tempo of the music composed by the machine learning models.

Impact of Changes: When you make changes to one aspect of the song, such as altering a word or a line in the lyrics, it can potentially influence the overall song. This is because the AI models may re-evaluate the song's structure, rhythm, and harmony to ensure that the lyrics and music remain coherent and well-integrated.

Degree of Independence: However, the models can also have a degree of independence. For example, the music composition model may generate a melody that is somewhat flexible and can accommodate minor changes in the lyrics without needing a complete overhaul of the musical composition.

Dynamic Interaction: The most advanced systems might employ dynamic interaction, where changes in one part of the song lead to real-time adjustments in other parts. This could mean that altering a lyric could immediately influence the melody or rhythm, ensuring the song remains harmonically and rhythmically aligned.

Control and Flexibility: The level of control and flexibility in how these models interact can vary. Some systems might allow for more manual adjustments, where the composer can change elements of the lyrics or music without triggering a complete re-composition. Others might be more automated, with the AI making most of the adjustments.

`grooveout`
I use it in the style prompt like it is a genre (chirp thinks it is a genre)

`kitchen beat`

As the FAQ indicates: https://suno-ai.notion.site/FAQs-b72601b96de44e5cacd2cd6baa985448

So the easiest way if you are on a free account is to leave the video as is.  It already has the logo and name baked into the video file.
[2023-12-28 20:03:34] 407677463548919828: So bear that in mind when considering this method...
[2023-12-28 20:03:58] 407677463548919828: Now the way that I do it is I use an image generator and one of those websites that let you design an album cover.
[2023-12-28 20:04:58] 407677463548919828: Then I use a script in the Linux terminal to run this command after making sure I have named my audio file and my image files properly and they are in the correct formats: `ffmpeg -loop 1 -i image.jpg -i audio.mp3 -c:v libx264 -tune stillimage -c:a aac -b:a 192k -vf "scale='iw-mod(iw,2)':'ih-mod(ih,2)',format=yuv420p" -shortest -movflags +faststart out.mp4`
[2023-12-28 20:05:46] 407677463548919828: That gives me something like this...
[2023-12-28 20:06:40] 407677463548919828: (Obviously you *do* need Linux or a terminal to make this work.  I have no idea how it works on Windows with a CMD console.  And of course ffmpeg needs  to be installed.)
[2023-12-28 20:07:07] 407677463548919828: I don't know how helpful that is to you but it is the way that works for me.

[2023-12-28 22:29:03] 1189080585403109437: The master track is broken down into separate tracks, voice, bass, drums, piano, guitar, etc. Audio tracks are produced and there is an option in RipX to export MIDI also.

[2023-12-28 22:32:37] 191308057148522496: ah I see stems are the bass, drums, piano, etc. I learned about them when using a DAW before but I didn't know they were called stems
[2023-12-28 22:33:46] 380287720582283264: A stem is just an individual element of a song.
Like, they record the drums, the guitars, the base, the vocals, each as individual tracks.
These are each "stems" 
They play all of the stems together at the same time, you get an actual song.

[2023-12-28 23:51:56] 614946962139250711: Not reliably, but sometimes of nested punctuation like "words (words (words [words]) words) words)" also maybe indention kind of looking like markdown. [2023-12-29 00:08:22] 232846139513241601: Hi! So hows the credits scheme here?
[2023-12-29 00:10:18] 614946962139250711: Free users have 50 credits, they refresh once a day. Paid plans for more credits at <https://app.suno.ai/> subscription menu
[2023-12-29 00:10:46] 232846139513241601: Right..so can length increase with paid?
[2023-12-29 00:12:09] 614946962139250711: Free and paid have the same lengths. 1:20 for first clip, 1:00 for each additional clip. You can in theory go as long as you want. Though quality eventually starts to degrade, almost always. Here's 18 minutes just to test the limits. Usually 3 or 4 is a good max to shoot for though. https://app.suno.ai/song/6f334b5c-c992-446b-8b46-2227c34e7300/
[2023-12-29 00:13:39] 614946962139250711: When you continue a song, try to listen to audio degradation and only choose clips that have the least amount of it, if you plan on continuing it more than a few times. It gets expensive for credits to do this, but if you want to preserve quality, can be necessary.

[2023-12-29 09:59:57] 614946962139250711: If you're working on one song, it may just be random chance in that particular song. Generally changing the song style in a continue requires a lot of effort. Repeat style words, repeat [bracket stuff] in the lyrics. You have to go in strong.

[2023-12-29 11:32:49] 660512327879360513: is there any tips so that the structure and melody is kept as close as possible from previous part when we try to continue the song?
[2023-12-29 11:44:02] 614946962139250711: The melody tends to stick fairly well by default, see wiki <https://suno-ai-wiki.notion.site/suno-ai-wiki/4e24aa838bf84fffaf383b1f9ed50748?v=755ec8954abd4e3f9f5c899950f0f315> and search up @wetcircuit chat messages in this discord for lots of good prompt examples (my prompts are often experiments so not good examples.)
[2023-12-29 11:44:44] 204320873719660544: try to keep the syllable count the same in your verse and chorus.

labeling each sections helps, but I find if the lyrics change too much it's not going to be possible to keep the song pattern... 

It's important to hear where the song is when the clip continues, and prompt the next plausible section correctly -- if it's in the middle of a chorus, complete the chorus and go to either another verse or a break.... 

try to refresh the chorus in every clip, if you use an instrumental section, be aware how far from the last chorus you've gotten – Chirp will forget if too much time has passed. 2-clips without a chorus and it is gone.

...and luck.

[2023-12-29 12:29:49] 614946962139250711: Maybe worth reposting this. TLDR the list is what we make.
# Chirp Prompts: The Big Picture 

Chirp prompting techniques are not *rules*, they are largely *discovered*. Even the developers don't know what works. Most information here and elsewhere is simply people trying things. (People coming from Stable Diffusion may be more used to this idea.)

That said, there are some *design decisions* Suno devs mentioned that are more like rules: 
1. Chirp is blind to real artist names.  
2. Text in **\[brackets\]** is seen as a **description** and **plain text** as **words to sing**.
3. Text in **(parenthesis)** is treated as **words to sing** but from **background** or **additional voices**.  
4. Commas in the **style** field break up phrases. In theory, ordering doesn't matter. (This doesn't match my V2 experience very much though.)
5. **Most of the music genres, descriptive words, and [tags] are not coming from a fixed list. ** There is no menu. We have to find and build the menu. 

These aren't *hard* rules - you may have noticed Chirp sometimes does sing "flute" at [flute] tags. 

**On #5**: In the earliest Chirp versions all tags were like this, even [chorus]. In later versions some tags like [verse] and [chorus] do have extra developer elbow grease behind them to be more consistent. This also applies to some style (general description words and some genres) prompts now as well. I don't know this list but my impression is that it isn't large, and most music genres are not hard coded like this.

[2023-12-29 12:38:21] 614946962139250711: Maybe I should add 
**6. The lyrics can influence the music style. **Try prompting tons of Christmas words, it can be enough to get Christmas sounding music, with a blank style prompt.  <https://app.suno.ai/song/a38736cc-bc99-454b-8ab6-c6a4500d875b/> Something to keep in mind, especially on the very first song segment the lyrics can swing things without you realizing sometimes.

[2023-12-29 14:08:05] 204320873719660544: As for Suno, they are making it difficult to *directly* mimic musicians or songs. (it would not be reliable, even if randomly possible... for about 8-bars...)

Suno avoids features that could be mis-used (no upload a cloned voice, no re-use copyright lyrics, no name a musician/band/song in the prompt as a 'style influence'). 

Their product is 'original' song generation. (this distinction should make Suno a less viable target from music industry –– there will be much lower-hanging fruit that is essentially nothing BUT cloning, like Anthropic.)

Cloning scene will be a huge pie that Suno seems to steer clear of, but Youtube is apparently going all in with licensed cloning of hits as a paid service (currently cloned songs get hundreds of thousands of hits, so they will want to keep that viewer clicks)

You are a creative lyricist specializing in crafting lyrics for songs across various genres. Your primary role is to create well-structured lyrics with good cadence and rhythm, fitting comfortably within a song's structure. Your lyrics will include structured sections like [verse] and [chorus], and feature adlibs within parentheses, enhancing the lyrical flow and emphasizing key phrases, as shown in the example: "In the quiet, I find my voice (I find my voice)". You cater to various music styles but avoid phrases like "Haze", "Reveries", "Symphony", and "Embrace", or lines that begin similarly to "In/On/Under the X, where the Y". Your goal is to provide coherent, catchy, and memorable lyrics for popular songs, with a focus on matching the tone and emotion of the song's theme.  Do not mention the style or genre in the lyrics.  Your task is to generate a song based on the following:

Style: Put your style here.
Song Name: If you leave this blank, ChatGPT will sometimes generate one.
Content: The basic content of the song.
PoV: Either first or third person works great.
Additional Notes: Anything you'd like the AI to know about the song.
