[2023-12-29 22:05:56] 831913934830174300: Can any staff tell me the difference b/w Continue and Remix?
 in how it effects the next generated music other that continue cmd joins the songs ahead.
[2023-12-29 22:06:47] 116357530111836160: Remix copies the prompt info over for you.  Continue tries to continue from where that clip ended.
[2023-12-29 22:07:27] 204320873719660544: Remix is just the text. copied to a new prompt...
(you need to have Custom switched on in the Create window)
[2023-12-29 22:07:59] 518974111863799809: Then if your done press whole clip
[2023-12-29 22:08:17] 518974111863799809: For the full thing

[2023-12-29 22:11:32] 831913934830174300: So distortion doesn continue if we generate song and use continue from there?
[2023-12-29 22:12:19] 116357530111836160: Distortion tends to slowly get worse as you continue more
[2023-12-29 22:12:21] 518974111863799809: It still continues but lower quality
[2023-12-29 22:15:17] 204320873719660544: it's kinda like a xerox of a xerox of a xerox... a little noise starts to build up into a lot of noise...

when you Continue from a clip, it listens to the end and continues what it hears... if the song happens to be a bit noisy there (reverb, guitar feedback, etc), that noise will continue as part of the overall sound in the next clip... (but a little worse each time)

[2023-12-29 23:00:53] 204320873719660544: here is an Ownership and Copyright page that does not answer that specific question... 🤕 

https://suno-ai.notion.site/FAQs-b72601b96de44e5cacd2cd6baa985448#47fc7b6ad1114a1597903b11c73ade77
[2023-12-29 23:12:34] 204320873719660544: So I will attempt to answer as I understand the TOS (and copyright law)

shortstory: with paid subscription we get commercial license and all rights to use the song 

when we submit our lyrics and prompts to Suno, we allow Suno to use our lyrics.... that includes letting other Suno subscribers build on a song with our lyrics in it, and they can use the song according to their license.

(this does not mean we get exclusive rights to our lyrics, we allow Suno to use them by agreeing to the service. To avoid the larger community access, we can use direct message on Discord, or keep our songs un-public and don't share the page address)

the person who wrote the lyric has copyright over THAT lyric. US Copyright Office is happy to cut apart any artwork into parts. They do not see the song as 1 thing, rather it is lyrics by person A, lyrics by person b, music by AI, art by another AI...

Essentially you can use the song, they can use the song. If someone wants to sue the other, it is unclear if either party would have any standing, based on agreeing to Suno's ToS.

So, cut that "500 songs" in half, since you're not very likely to use both versions of the same snippet.  Then the average song is 3:15 (195 secs) so it's going to take about four to five snippets to actually create a full-length song.

That 250 snippets now only really yields 50-60 songs.

Even if you're only happy with a quarter of those, it seems like a great value.

I just appreciate up-front transparency.

free subscriptions get non-commercial license and must give attribution to Suno (Suno owns the song)

[2023-12-30 18:26:49] 723279639149150278: what does the remix button in the website ?
[2023-12-30 18:28:25] 204320873719660544: yes... it's a 'trick' of the UI that isn't well explained...

for clip 2, you Continue From.. clip 1
for clip 3, you Continue From.. clip 2
for clip 4, you Continue From.. clip 3
... and so on, daisy chaining on after the other.

you must update the Continue From clip, or you are just making Clip 2 over and over...
[2023-12-30 18:30:23] 204320873719660544: when you have the Custom mode switched on, you get 2 prompting windows, one for Lyrics, one for Style/genre...

the Remix button will copy that clips text prompts, and past them into the Custom prompt windows...

(it's just the text, copy-paste, but the Custom mode needs to be switched on or nothing happens)
[2023-12-30 18:30:36] 356164432293789696: like a dying android sheep, o m g.  This one is 1000x better w/ the same lyrics: https://youtube.com/shorts/LAOZUZH_QNQ?si=5la2ogfwS9Rpd-5T

[2023-12-30 19:01:15] 1171082285664178206: Thanks wetcircuit. I'm still not sure how to make sure the finished song will have the "complete" song in the time allotted, if that makes sense?
[2023-12-30 19:13:00] 204320873719660544: Chirp will start to offer  endings to your song... maybe one clip is the full 60sec and the music continues into the next chirp..., but the other clip (totally random) will be 37sec because the song has an ending in this clip.... And of course can regenerate the clips if neither are what you want.

on a clip that ends you can click [...] and choose Get Whole Song... and it will stitch all the clips in the chain into 1 longer song (free, no tokens). You can also stitch up a song in-progress to refresh your memory how it's flowing...

The problem is getting the song to end when WE want it to end.... Some genres make really short songs and will give a lot of endings (we have to instruct it NOT to end, by prompting for [instrumental] or arranging the verse chorus prompt so it isn't ignored... (filling up the lyrics so it doesn't end early)

The reverse is trying to get a song to end when it doesn't want to (Jazz Piano and Disco can literally go on and on forever) so then we have to start prompting for [Outro] or [Fade Out] or [End]....

I think that was your question...?

[2023-12-30 20:35:47] 116357530111836160: You don't own a copyright on things you didn't write
[2023-12-30 20:36:30] 116357530111836160: If you modify something from an AI, it *might* qualify, but that's still very iffy
[2023-12-30 20:37:41] 116357530111836160: The portions you do would qualify, assuming it's enough to copyright anyway, and the combination of what you write and the AI work may qualify, but the AI stuff on it's onw definitely does not
[2023-12-30 20:39:22] 116357530111836160: The legality of AI stuff is still being fought over, but that's the most on-point result I'm aware of

[2023-12-31 02:53:37] 229455161888735243: suno does *not* like you continuing the same portion you were previously doing
[2023-12-31 02:54:46] 407677463548919828: > Try depending on where you are in your lyrics (if you have a lot more to go) just continuing and running the same lyrics you have.  This can sometimes make it pick up where it left off.  A large percentage of the time it will start over in the middle of the song.  This is just the easiest thing to try to see if it works.
> 
> Next try removing everything already sung but the last good line, then try continuing.  This sometimes works.  It's how it always seemed to work in v1 Chirp for me.
> 
> After this try creating a line break (empty space) and continuing from the line that was interrupted.  This has a good chance of working.
> 
> Then starting with the line that was broken.  It sometimes will "remember" where it was and pick up from there.
> 
> Finally when all else fails, batten down the hatches, remove everything up to the word that was interrupted and hope for the best and throw the dice.  You will likely encounter some weirdness and you will need to keep trying.  But if everything else has failed it's this or...
> 
> ...or you go back up a chain in the song and try again giving up on the good part.  It hurts, I know, I know.  But sometimes it's the only way forward.
> 
> ...As you can probably tell this is a bit of a pain point for me and I've done these often...

[2023-12-31 23:02:18] 116357530111836160: You can put pretty much anything and it will *try* to make sense of it
[2023-12-31 23:03:24] 116357530111836160: Jonathan Fly posted a song in the genre of PerpendicularCheesePizza https://app.suno.ai/song/d35a07cf-78b5-413c-9b79-c471975906e0/
[2023-12-31 23:03:49] 204320873719660544: I think it does well when you put a song type like Anime opening, or heartbreak movie theme -- I got really good results with TV Mystery Theme...

then the genre, and some details...

I think you actually hit a winning formula... saying what the song is for, before describing it.

Seems to make more 'complete' songs that have more energy and composition (as opposed to repeating beats or riff that can happen if I describe instruments or genre first)
[2023-12-31 23:04:20] 204320873719660544: (but this is very anecdotal... )
[2023-12-31 23:05:06] 380287720582283264: Best advice I can give you is to just experiment.
Try anything that tickles your fancy.
See if it has any effect or not.
Sometimes you'll succeed, sometimes it won't...
FIguring it all out is part of the fun.
[2023-12-31 23:06:02] 204320873719660544: It's always a joke song that turns out shockingly beautiful...
[2023-12-31 23:06:25] 116357530111836160: Describing the mood of a song along with the genre tends to do a lot to direct it.
[2023-12-31 23:07:34] 204320873719660544: moods are like everything...

also give the chorus and verses a mood

[dramatic verse]
[Sinister chorus]
[2023-12-31 23:07:43] 116357530111836160: "Aggressive triumphant metal" gives it a much better idea what the song should sound like than just asking for metal and hoping it get's the mood.
[2023-12-31 23:08:22] 204320873719660544: [Aggressive verse]
[Triumphant chorus]
[2023-12-31 23:09:19] 380287720582283264: [*brief* interlude]  works to stop the interlude from taking over the full clip, if you want to keep things short
[2023-12-31 23:11:43] 283341731666329611: Happy new year to those from the Netherlands and neighbors
[2023-12-31 23:12:51] 204320873719660544: Give Chirp a [Catchy Hook] with very simple words repeated and it will make songs that get stuck in your head, aka: A HIT FACTORY!

put that hook everywhere... at the start... after the chorus... at the end...
